meanings.definition: formulation of the plans and important details
a framework that supports and protects a picture or a mirror
examples: the frame enhances but is not itself the subject of attention
the frame was much more valuable than the miror it held

meanings.definition: enclose in or as if in a frame
enclose in a frame, as of a picture
take or catch as if in a snare or trap
examples: The innocent man was framed by the police

meanings.definition: formulate in a particular style or language
examples: I wouldn't put it that way
She cast her request in very polite language

meanings.definition: make up plans or basic details for
construct by fitting or uniting parts together
